content:
# Matthew Chapter 12 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_012_page_001.jpg, MAT_012_page_002.jpg, MAT_012_page_003.jpg

### Lord of the Sabbath

1. Around that time, Jesus was walking through some grainfields on the Sabbath. His disciples were hungry and started picking heads of grain to eat.

2. The Pharisees saw this and said, "Look! Your disciples are breaking the Sabbath law!"

3. Jesus replied, "Haven't you read what David did when he and his companions were hungry?

4. He entered the house of God and ate the sacred bread — which was forbidden for anyone except the priests.

5. And haven't you read in the Law that the priests in the temple work on the Sabbath and yet are innocent?

6. I tell you, something greater than the temple is here.

7. If you understood what this means — 'I want mercy, not sacrifice' — you wouldn't condemn the innocent.

8. For the Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath."

### Healing on the Sabbath

9. Moving on, Jesus entered their synagogue.

10. A man with a paralyzed hand was there. Looking for a reason to charge Jesus, they asked him, "Is it legal to heal on the Sabbath?"

11. He answered, "If one of you has a sheep and it falls into a ditch on the Sabbath, wouldn't you grab it and pull it out?

12. A human being is worth far more than a sheep! So yes — it is perfectly legal to do good on the Sabbath."

13. Then he told the man, "Hold out your hand." The man held it out, and it was completely restored, as healthy as the other.

14. The Pharisees walked out and began plotting how to kill him.

### The Chosen Servant

15. Aware of their plans, Jesus left that area. Large crowds followed him, and he healed everyone who was sick,

16. but he warned them not to publicize who he was.

17. This fulfilled what the prophet Isaiah had spoken:

18. "Here is my servant, my chosen one, the one I love, in whom I delight. I will place my Spirit on him, and he will announce justice to the nations.

19. He won't argue or shout; no one will hear him raising his voice in the streets.

20. He won't snap a broken reed or snuff out a flickering candle — not until he leads justice to triumph.

21. And the nations will put their hope in his name."

### The Beelzebul Accusation

22. Then a man who was demon-possessed, blind, and unable to speak was brought to Jesus. He healed the man so that he could both talk and see.

23. The whole crowd was stunned. "Could this really be the Son of David?" they asked.

24. But when the Pharisees heard this, they said, "He's only driving out demons because Beelzebul, the prince of demons, gives him power."

25. Jesus knew what they were thinking and said, "Any kingdom at war with itself collapses. Any city or household torn apart by conflict can't survive.

26. If Satan is driving out Satan, he's fighting against himself. How can his kingdom last?

27. And if I'm driving out demons by Beelzebul, what power are your own people using when they do the same thing? They'll be your judges on that point.

28. But if I'm driving out demons by the Spirit of God, then the kingdom of God has arrived at your doorstep.

29. How can anyone break into a strong man's house and steal his possessions unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can rob the house.

30. Whoever isn't with me is against me, and whoever isn't helping me gather is scattering.

31. So I'm telling you: every sin and blasphemy can be forgiven — but blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven.

32. Anyone who speaks against the Son of Man can be forgiven. But anyone who speaks against the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven, not in this age or the next.

### Trees and Their Fruit

33. A tree is identified by its fruit. If a tree is healthy, its fruit will be good. If a tree is diseased, its fruit will be bad.

34. You nest of snakes! How can evil people say anything good? Whatever fills your heart is what overflows from your mouth.

35. A good person produces good things from the goodness stored inside, and an evil person produces evil things from the evil stored inside.

36. And I tell you this: on judgment day, people will have to answer for every thoughtless word they've spoken.

37. Your own words will either acquit you or condemn you."

### The Sign of Jonah

38. Then some of the scribes and Pharisees said to him, "Teacher, we'd like to see you perform a miraculous sign."

39. Jesus replied, "Only an evil, unfaithful generation demands a sign! The only sign you'll get is the sign of the prophet Jonah.

40. For just as Jonah spent three days and three nights in the belly of the great sea creature, the Son of Man will spend three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.

41. The people of Nineveh will stand up against this generation on judgment day and condemn it, because they turned from their sins when Jonah preached — and something greater than Jonah is here now.

42. The queen of the South will rise up against this generation on judgment day and condemn it, because she traveled from the ends of the earth to hear Solomon's wisdom — and something greater than Solomon is here now.

### The Return of an Evil Spirit

43. When an evil spirit leaves a person, it wanders through arid wastelands looking for a place to rest, but finds nothing.

44. Then it says, 'I'll go back to the house I left.' When it returns, it finds the place vacant, clean, and all in order.

45. So it goes and rounds up seven other spirits even more evil than itself, and they all move in and settle down. The person ends up worse off than before. That's what will happen to this evil generation."

### Jesus' True Family

46. While Jesus was still talking to the crowd, his mother and brothers arrived and stood outside, wanting to speak with him.

47. Someone told him, "Your mother and brothers are out here and want to talk to you."

48. Jesus replied, "Who is my mother? Who are my brothers?"

49. He gestured toward his disciples and said, "Look — here are my mother and my brothers.

50. Anyone who does the will of my Father in heaven is my brother and my sister and my mother."

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verse 6 — "something greater than the temple":** This is an enormous claim. The temple was the center of Jewish identity, the dwelling place of God on earth. Jesus says something greater has arrived — not just someone, but something: the kingdom of God, embodied in his person and mission.

**Verse 20 — "a broken reed... a flickering candle":** Isaiah's servant poem (42:1-4) describes someone who handles the damaged and the fragile with extreme care. This is Matthew's portrait of Jesus: not a political revolutionary crushing opposition, but a healer who protects what's barely surviving.

**Verses 31-32 — The unforgivable sin:** The context is crucial. The Pharisees have just witnessed an undeniable work of God's Spirit — a blind, mute, demon-possessed man healed — and attributed it to Satan. Blasphemy against the Spirit isn't a careless word or a moment of doubt. It's the deliberate, clear-eyed rejection of God's work when you can see it right in front of you.

**Verse 40 — "three days and three nights":** The sign of Jonah is the only sign Jesus will give. Jonah's descent into the sea creature and emergence after three days prefigures Jesus' death and resurrection. The "heart of the earth" parallels "the belly of the great sea creature."

**Verses 46-50 — True family:** Jesus doesn't reject his biological family, but he redefines what family means. His true kin are those who share his mission — doing the Father's will. This redefinition of kinship is radical in an honor-shame culture where family loyalty was paramount.

— PNEUMA, AI Translator
